ayahArabic: لَعَلَّنَا نَتَّبِعُ ٱلسَّحَرَةَ إِن كَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْغٰلِبِينَ
ayahEnglish: That we might follow the magicians if they are the predominant?"

ayahArabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالُوا۟ لِفِرْعَوْنَ أَئِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجْرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحْنُ ٱلْغٰلِبِينَ
ayahEnglish: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"

ayahArabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالُوا۟ لِفِرْعَوْنَ أَئِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجْرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحْنُ ٱلْغٰلِبِينَ
ayahEnglish: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"

targetTranslation: is it indeed (that we will have a reward?)
ayahArabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالُوا۟ لِفِرْعَوْنَ أَئِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجْرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحْنُ ٱلْغٰلِبِينَ
ayahEnglish: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"

targetTranslation: we were (the subject of قَالُوا۟ - they said)
ayahArabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالُوا۟ لِفِرْعَوْنَ أَئِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجْرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحْنُ ٱلْغٰلِبِينَ
ayahEnglish: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"

ayahEnglish: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"

ayahArabic: قَالَ نَعَمْ وَإِنَّكُمْ إِذًا لَّمِنَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبِينَ
ayahEnglish: He said, "Yes, and indeed, you will then be of those near [to me]."

ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ
ayahEnglish: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."

ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ
ayahEnglish: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."

ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ
ayahEnglish: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."

targetTranslation: what (asking about أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ - what you are throwing)
ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ
ayahEnglish: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."

ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ
ayahEnglish: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."

targetTranslation: those who will throw/cast (in response to موسى's command)
ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ
ayahEnglish: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."

ayahArabic: فَأَلْقَوْا۟ حِبَالَهُمْ وَعِصِيَّهُمْ وَقَالُوا۟ بِعِزَّةِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ ٱلْغٰلِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: So they threw their ropes and their staffs and said, "By the might of Pharaoh, indeed it is we who are predominant."

targetTranslation: Pharaoh (as the object being acted upon in بِعِزَّةِ فِرْعَوْنَ - by the might of Pharaoh)
ayahArabic: فَأَلْقَوْا۟ حِبَالَهُمْ وَعِصِيَّهُمْ وَقَالُوا۟ بِعِزَّةِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ ٱلْغٰلِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: So they threw their ropes and their staffs and said, "By the might of Pharaoh, indeed it is we who are predominant."

ayahArabic: فَأَلْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَلْقَفُ مَا يَأْفِكُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.

ayahArabic: فَأَلْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَلْقَفُ مَا يَأْفِكُونَ

ayahEnglish: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.

targetTranslation: they turn away from truth/deceive (referring to the magicians' deceptive tricks)

ayahEnglish: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.

ayahArabic: فَأُلْقِىَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سٰجِدِينَ
ayahEnglish: So the magicians fell down in prostration [to Allāh].

ayahArabic: قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ ٱلْعٰلَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "We have believed in the Lord of the worlds,

targetTranslation: we believed (in response to what was shown to them)
ayahArabic: قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ ٱلْعٰلَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "We have believed in the Lord of the worlds,

ayahArabic: رَبِّ مُوسَىٰ وَهٰرُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Lord of Moses and Aaron."

ayahArabic: رَبِّ مُوسَىٰ وَهٰرُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Lord of Moses and Aaron."

ayahArabic: قَالَ ءَامَنتُمْ لَهُۥ قَبْلَ أَنْ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمْ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِى عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحْرَ فَلَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُم مِّنْ خِلٰفٍ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
ayahEnglish: [Pharaoh] said, "You believed him [i.e., Moses] before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic, but you are going to know. I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you all."

ayahEnglish: They said, "No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return.

targetTranslation: harm (object of لا meaning 'there is absolutely no harm')

ayahEnglish: They said, "No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return.

targetTranslation: those who are returning (to رَبِّنَا - our Lord)
ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ لَا ضَيْرَ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا مُنقَلِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return.

targetTranslation: we hope (for أَن يَغْفِرَ - that He will forgive)
ayahArabic: إِنَّا نَطْمَعُ أَن يَغْفِرَ لَنَا رَبُّنَا خَطٰيٰنَآ أَن كُنَّآ أَوَّلَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, we aspire that our Lord will forgive us our sins because we were the first of the believers."

ayahEnglish: And We inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants; indeed, you will be pursued."

targetTranslation: travel by night! (command to one person)
ayahArabic: وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبَادِىٓ إِنَّكُم مُّتَّبَعُونَ

ayahArabic: فَأَرْسَلَ فِرْعَوْنُ فِى ٱلْمَدَآئِنِ حٰشِرِينَ
ayahEnglish: Then Pharaoh sent among the cities gatherers

ayahEnglish: Then Pharaoh sent among the cities gatherers

ayahArabic: إِنَّ هٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَشِرْذِمَةٌ قَلِيلُونَ
ayahEnglish: [And said], "Indeed, those are but a small band,

targetTranslation: those who are cautious (part of جَمِيعٌ حٰذِرُونَ 'all cautious/alert')

ayahArabic: قَالَ رَبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: He said, "My Lord is most knowing of what you do."

ayahArabic: قَالَ رَبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ

ayahArabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَهُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمِ ٱلظُّلَّةِ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And they denied him, so the punishment of the day of the black cloud seized them. Indeed, it was the punishment of a terrible day.

ayahEnglish: In a clear Arabic language.

ayahEnglish: And he had recited it to them [perfectly], they would [still] not have been believers in it.

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ سَلَكْنٰهُ فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ

targetTranslation: they believe/have faith (present tense) (subject of لَا)

ayahEnglish: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.

ayahArabic: لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ

ayahArabic: فَيَأْتِيَهُم بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And it will come to them suddenly while they perceive [it] not.

ayahEnglish: And We did not destroy any city except that it had warners

ayahArabic: وَمَآ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا لَهَا مُنذِرُونَ

ayahArabic: ذِكْرَىٰ وَمَا كُنَّا ظٰلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: As a reminder; and never have We been unjust.

ayahArabic: ذِكْرَىٰ وَمَا كُنَّا ظٰلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: As a reminder; and never have We been unjust.

ayahEnglish: And the devils have not brought it [i.e., the revelation] down.

ayahArabic: وَمَا يَنۢبَغِى لَهُمْ وَمَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
ayahEnglish: It is not allowable for them, nor would they be able.

ayahArabic: وَمَا يَنۢبَغِى لَهُمْ وَمَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ

ayahArabic: فَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُعَذَّبِينَ
ayahEnglish: So do not invoke with Allāh another deity and [thus] be among the punished.

ayahArabic: فَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُعَذَّبِينَ
ayahEnglish: So do not invoke with Allāh another deity and [thus] be among the punished.

ayahArabic: فَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُعَذَّبِينَ
ayahEnglish: So do not invoke with Allāh another deity and [thus] be among the punished.

ayahEnglish: So do not invoke with Allāh another deity and [thus] be among the punished.

ayahArabic: وَأَنذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ ٱلْأَقْرَبِينَ

ayahArabic: وَٱخْفِضْ جَنَاحَكَ لِمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

ayahEnglish: And if they disobey you, then say, "Indeed, I am disassociated from what you are doing."

ayahArabic: تَنَزَّلُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ أَفَّاكٍ أَثِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: They descend upon every sinful liar.

targetTranslation: they throw (the subject of يُلْقُونَ ٱلسَّمْعَ - they cast their hearing)
ayahArabic: يُلْقُونَ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ كٰذِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: They pass on what is heard, and most of them are liars.

ayahArabic: يُلْقُونَ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ كٰذِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: They pass on what is heard, and most of them are liars.

targetTranslation: he follows them intensely (the misguided ones follow the poets)
ayahArabic: وَٱلشُّعَرَآءُ يَتَّبِعُهُمُ ٱلْغَاوُۥنَ
ayahEnglish: And the poets - [only] the deviators follow them;

targetTranslation: have you not...? (addressing the Prophet ﷺ)
ayahArabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍ يَهِيمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Do you not see that in every valley they roam

targetTranslation: you see (after أَلَمْ meaning 'do you not see?')

ayahEnglish: And that they say what they do not do? -

targetTranslation: that which (connects يَقُولُونَ with لَا يَفْعَلُونَ)

ayahArabic: وَأَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ مَا لَا يَفْعَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: And that they say what they do not do? -

targetTranslation: those who (plural, refers to believers in ءَامَنُوا۟)
ayahArabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّالِحٰتِ وَذَكَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًا وَٱنتَصَرُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا ظُلِمُوا۟ وَسَيَعْلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَىَّ مُنقَلَبٍ يَنقَلِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Except those [poets] who believe and do righteous deeds and remember Allāh often and defend [the Muslims] after they were wronged. And those who have wronged are going to know to what [kind of] return they will be returned.
